item.dc:creator: deafkitten

item.title: Can Imgur help me in remembering my brother?
item.description:
I lost my brother last weekend... He was severely disabled with epilepsy, but he was always happy and laughing. His condition was chronic so over the years he lost the ability to walk and talk. And yet he was always full of joy; grinning and laughing when you would talk to him. He would smack things with his hand as a way of interacting with his surroundings so we would have lots of fun doing high-fives.
Its hard looking back on all the memories, but I know its really important to process through the grief. I know Imgur is a big mix of lots of beliefs and backgrounds; my family is Christian and it helps to picture him in Heaven in a healed and healthy body. When he was in hospice, I kept imagining him being able to run through green fields and play and laugh. The Bible talks about the Earth being redeemed and being made perfect again (we can all agree things are pretty messed up now, right) so its nice to think about him enjoying Heaven by doing all the things he wasn't able to in this life.
There are so many talented artists on here so I was hoping I could get some help with making a memorial picture/painting of him. I have this image of him laughing and running through green, grassy fields. Would any artistic imgurians be willing to help me make a painting that I can remember him by? I have included some pictures from his life that I hope will help. If more would be useful, I am currently going through our old photos and making digital copies. Hopefully, I will have more soon. Thanks in advance
See you on the other side Mathew! EDIT: Wow, thank you everyone for your support and kind words! I am so happy to see that Matthew's smile continues to reach out and brighten people's day even after he is gone! Its so heartwarming to know that this community can carry on his memory!
item.dc:creator: chillychiweenie

item.title: Okayama, Japan.
item.description: Okayama is the capital city of it's namesake prefecture, in western Japan. It’s known for the 16th-century Okayama Castle, dubbed “Crow Castle” for its black exterior, and for Korakuen, an iconic formal garden. The Hayashibara Museum of Art shows East Asian art and artifacts. The Okayama Prefectural Museum of Art displays regional works. To the west, 15th-century Kibitsu-jinja Shrine features a long, covered walkway.
item.dc:creator: dolphinyaso

In fact, it’s so old that I think the hard drive's gonna break soon, it keeps beeping at me. Better complete these back ups quick.

item.dc:creator: sprxsie

item.title: Electricity is "a mystery"
item.description: There's a typo. As a previous layout designer, I know a simple spell check, offered by all industry-standard software regardless of version, only takes a second. Must have been made in Microsoft Publisher.
item.dc:creator: MyCommentsUsuallyHaveTypos

item.title: Panic Attack vs Anxiety Attackl
item.description: for some reason, it was really comforting to me to find something that really describes the differences between the two distraught states FP Edit: this is not a diagnosis. symptoms can/do vary, overlap, and intermix person to person and even attack to attack. i posted it with the intent of helping people a) know they’re not alone, crazy, making it up, exaggerating, etc b) better distinguish and describe the physical sensations to a medical professional. better discussion about the problem = better, more effective treatment plan (ideally, anyways). also, sorry for the typo in the title. ironically it’s giving me a bit of anxiety at the thought of people -even total strangers- might be judging me for it!
item.dc:creator: stonedlikeicheckedoutmedusa

item.title: Your dare interrupt happy cheese time?!
item.description:
My dude.
It’s Wednesday, my dudes. Edit: Thank you all again, really. I know it’s just “fake internet points” and having another post become Most Viral doesn’t mean much, but it does to me. As a fellow “outlier looking in” I’ve always had trouble connecting with people, memes help. Be safe, sleep happy, and remember - you are loved.
item.dc:creator: theRebecinator

item.title: Good things are happening. Russians tried to cross Siverskyi Donets river. It did not go so well. 73 equipment annihilated. Typical Russian, thats not the first time they tried to cross this river at the exact location.
